nodes.name: 家八哥
nodes.summary: 家八哥（Common myna，学名 Acridotheres tristis），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联27个分布地点节点。
nodes.shortSummary: 家八哥（学名：Acridotheres tristis），俗稱禽吉了，为椋鸟科八哥属的鸟类，原產於中亞、南亞和中國，後來引入美洲、非洲等世界其他地區。 家八哥是雜食性鳥類，喜歡開放的樹林，並且有強烈的領地意識，對城市地區環境適應良好。

nodes.name: 斯里蘭卡
nodes.summary: 斯里蘭卡是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联59种鸟类，包括蓝孔雀、Painted francolin、大红鹳等59种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 脊索动物门

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The common myna or Indian myna (Acridotheres tristis), sometimes spelled mynah, is a bird in the family Sturnidae, native to Asia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The common myna or Indian myna (Acridotheres tristis), sometimes spelled mynah, is a bird in the family Sturnidae, native to Asia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|220x220px|Fighting, Madagascar The common myna is native to Asia, with its initial home range spanning Iran, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Sri Lanka, Afghanistan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Myanmar, Malaysia, Singapore, peninsular Thailand, Indochina, Japan (both mainland Japan and the Ryukyu Islands) and China. thumb|left|A common myna as an invasive species in Gülhane Park, Turkey thumb|Visiting a home garden in Colombo, [[Sri Lanka]]The common myna has been introduced to many other parts of the world such as Canada, Australia, Turkey, Israel, New Zealand, New Caledonia, Fiji, the United States (South Florida only), South Africa, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Cayman Islands, Islands in the Indian Ocean (the Seychelles – from which it was subsequently eradicated at great expense,{{Cite journal| journal= PLOS ONE|first1=Thomas|last1=Evans|first2=Elena|last2=Angulo|first3=Corey J

links.evidence: thumb|220x220px|Fighting, Madagascar The common myna is native to Asia, with its initial home range spanning Iran, Pakistan, India, Nepal, Bhutan, Bangladesh and Sri Lanka, Afghanistan, Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Myanmar, Malaysia, Singapore, peninsular Thailand, Indochina, Japan (both mainland Japan and the Ryukyu Islands) and China. thumb|left|A common myna as an invasive species in Gülhane Park, Turkey thumb|Visiting a home garden in Colombo, [[Sri Lanka]]The common myna has been introduced to many other parts of the world such as Canada, Australia, Turkey, Israel, New Zealand, New Caledonia, Fiji, the United States (South Florida only), South Africa, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Uzbekistan, Cayman Islands, Islands in the Indian Ocean (the Seychelles – from which it was subsequently eradicated at great expense,{{Cite journal| journal= PLOS ONE|first1=Thomas|last1=Evans|first2=Elena|last2=Angulo|first3=Corey J

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: A.|last3=Bradshaw|first4=Anna|last4=Turbelin|first5=Franck|last5=Courchamp|doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0292854|title=Global economic costs of alien birds|date=2023 |volume=18 |issue=10 |article-number=e0292854 |doi-access=free|pmid=37851652 |pmc=10584179|bibcode=2023PLoSO..1892854E }} Mauritius, Réunion, Madagascar, the Maldives, the Andaman and Nicobar Islands and the Lakshadweep archipelago) and also in islands of the Atlantic (such as Ascension and Saint Helena), Pacific Ocean and Cyprus, as of February 2022. The range of the common myna is increasing to the extent that in 2000 the IUCN Species Survival Commission declared it among 100 of the world's worst invasive species. It is typically found in open woodland, cultivation and around habitation
